summary.intent: Cut and push a new release tag (20260414.01) for the moltis project and verify CI passes.
summary.outcome: CI failed on the initial tag due to an outdated E2E test password; the old tag was deleted, the fix was confirmed already committed, and a new tag was created and pushed from the corrected HEAD.

repo: Password validation enforces a 12-character minimum — test credentials must meet this requirement.
Release process involves tagging, CI artifact build, checksum generation, GitHub release creation, and a separate manual GPG-signing step requiring a YubiKey tap.
CHANGELOG.md and website/changelog/index.html are both updated as part of the release prep commit.

code.finding: E2E test was using 'testpass123' (11 chars) which failed the 12-char minimum password validation; fixed to 'testpass1234'.

workflow: When a tag is pushed before all fixes are committed, the tag must be deleted locally and remotely then re-created from the corrected HEAD rather than amending.
CI failure on a release tag does not necessarily mean new work is needed — the fix may already exist in a subsequent commit.

friction: Release tag was pushed before a known test fix was included in the tagged commit, requiring a full tag deletion and re-tag cycle.
CI E2E failure created ambiguity about whether the fix had already been applied, requiring manual log inspection and commit history review to confirm.
open_items: GPG-signing of release artifacts (requires YubiKey tap) — run `./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh 20260414.01` once CI completes on the new tag.
